perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information includes, but is not limited to, financial or other competitive business information that may be collected by the Association regarding industry members (and non-members) of the Association, information of a personal nature regarding any of th e officers, staff, or employees of the Association or its members, information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions (unless such information has been specifically cleared for public release), and any other information which might be reasonably foreseen to be harmful if disclosed outside of the Association and/or for reasons other than in accordance with a D irector’s, Officer’s, or Staff Member ’s duties to the Association. In the case that the Recipient is unclear or has any question regarding whether or not a particular piece of information falls within Confidential Information, the Recipient will consult with the Executive Director or Office Manager prior to disclosing such information.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information includes only financial data and sensitive personal information of officers and staff. Information shared in Board meetings is excluded from being considered confidential. Any uncertainty about whether specific data is confidential should be clarified with the Executive Director before disclosure; however, Executive Director decisions are not final.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by narrowing the definition of 'Confidential Information'. Originally, it included a broad range of data. The change restricts it to only financial and personal data and explicitly excludes Board discussions. The phrase 'Executive Director decisions are not final' creates in-text contradiction and uncertainty about who determines what is confidential and under what conditions, conflicting with other sections outlining clear confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (4) that the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members of the Association, unless he/she has clear instruction from the Executive Director or Board Chairperson to do so, and then shall disclose such information only for the limited purpose for which such instruction is given and no other purpose;
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of Confidential Information, excluding Board discussions. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Recipient needs instruction from the Executive Director or Board Chairperson to disclose confidential information, which now excludes board discussions under the new definition. Furthermore, the addition of 'Executive Director decisions are not final' creates uncertainty and conflicts with the contradicted text, which implies the Executive Director's instruction is sufficient for disclosure. Thus, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is entered into as of _______________, ____ between __________________________ (“Recipient”) and the International Code of Conduct for Private Security Service Providers’ Association (the “ICoCA” or the “Association ”)(together the Parties).
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is entered into as of _______________, ____ between __________________________ (“Recipient”) and the International Code of Conduct for Private Security Service Providers’ Association (the “ICoCA” or the “Association ”)(together the Parties). This agreement shall not be valid for any person other than a Recipient and an ICoCA representative.
perturbation.explanation: By stating 'This agreement shall not be valid for any person other than a Recipient and an ICoCA representative' creates an in-text contradiction because 'members' are referenced later in the text as an entity subject to the agreement. This creates uncertainty in how the NDA applies to third-party members of the association.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (1) that in the normal course of business of the Association, there may be times when Recipient, as a Director, Officer, or Staff Member of the Association, receives information that is confidential, sensitive, and/or not in the public domain and that, if disclosed to persons other than those for whom the information as intended, has the potential to harm the Association, its individual members, other participants, or any of their business partners or related entities or persons (such information her eafter referred to as “Confidential Information”);
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a limitation stating the agreement is only valid for the Recipient and an ICoCA representative. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which mentions the agreement's potential impact on 'individual members' of the Association. The addition in the Changed Text creates an inconsistency, as members are now excluded whereas the original text contemplates that they are subjected to the agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: that in the event Recipient violates any part of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement, the Association may pursue legal and/or equitable remedies from Recipient; (7) that in the event any proceeding to enforce this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement or decide any matter relating to it Recipient becomes necessary, the Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Switzerland pertaining thereto and the Recipient agrees to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Canton of Geneva, Switzerland
perturbation.changed_text: that in the event Recipient violates any part of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement, the Association may pursue legal and/or equitable remedies from Recipient; (7) that in the event any proceeding to enforce this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement or decide any matter relating to it Recipient becomes necessary, the Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Recipient's country pertaining thereto and the Recipient agrees to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Recipient's country.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the governing law and jurisdiction. Originally, the agreement was governed by Swiss law and the courts of Geneva. The perturbation changes this so the agreement is governed and has exclusive jurisdiction by the Recipient's country. This leads to an in-text contradiction on which law and jurisdiction should be used, especially if the Recipient is not in Switzerland.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (7) that in the event any proceeding to enforce this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement or decide any matter relating to it Recipient becomes necessary, the Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Recipient's country pertaining thereto and the Recipient agrees to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Recipient's country;
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement is governed by the laws of Switzerland and the courts of the Canton of Geneva. The changed text alters this by stating that the agreement is governed by the laws of the Recipient's country and the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Recipient's country. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text. This change creates a direct contradiction regarding which country's laws and jurisdiction apply, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: that the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members of the Association, unless he/she has clear instruction from the Executive Director or Board Chairperson to do so, and then shall disclose such information only for the limited purpose for which such instruction is given and no other purpose
perturbation.changed_text: that the Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to any person, including those outside the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members of the Association, if such disclosure is deemed necessary for the advancement of the Association's goals, as determined by the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction by giving the Recipient the authority to disclose confidential information to *any* person if they deem it 'necessary for the advancement of the Association's goals.' This directly contradicts the original restriction that limits disclosure to only Directors, Officers, or Staff Members and only with specific instruction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (4) that the Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to any person, including those outside the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members of the Association, if such disclosure is deemed necessary for the advancement of the Association's goals, as determined by the Recipient;
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Recipient to disclose confidential information to 'any person' if they deem it necessary, directly contradicting the original text and implicitly, the contradicted text, which initially restricted disclosure to only Directors, Officers, or Staff Members with specific instructions. This introduces a strong inconsistency regarding who can access confidential information, making the contract unenforceable on the Confidentiality clause.

perturbation.original_text: and (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer, the violation of which will permit the Association to terminate such employment or terminate such person from his7her position in addition to seeking any other legal or equitable remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: and (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is encouraged, but not an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer. Violation of this agreement will not lead to termination, but may result in a warning from the Association.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction by stating that adherence to the NDA is now 'encouraged, but not an essential term,' and that violations will not lead to termination. This directly opposes the original clause which stated adherence was 'essential' and violations 'will permit the Association to terminate.' This creates confusion about the consequences of violating the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is encouraged, but not an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer. Violation of this agreement will not lead to termination, but may result in a warning from the Association.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text states that adherence to the NDA is 'encouraged, but not an essential term' and that violations 'will not lead to termination, but may result in a warning.' This directly contradicts the original text, which stated adherence was 'essential' and violations 'will permit the Association to terminate.' Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, the contradiction exists and is strong, as the consequences for violating the NDA are now fundamentally different, creating a clear conflict within the document.
